description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The exterior features a mix of red brick and beige siding, with the front and one side clad in brick and the other sides in siding. The house has multiple rectangular windows with white frames, arranged in an asymmetrical pattern. A small covered porch with steps and a simple railing marks the main entryway, which is slightly recessed and supported by posts. The house sits on a rectangular plot of green grass, bordered by a dark brown base, suggesting a foundation or raised platform. The overall design is practical and modest, typical of North American residential neighborhoods.
description: A two-story suburban house with a gable roof, red brick and beige siding, and a small covered porch entryway. The windows are white-framed and asymmetrically arranged, and the house sits on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, three windows, and the red brick facade.
description_view_1: Side view with beige siding, three windows, and the porch visible on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view with a brick facade, two windows, and the porch on the left side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with beige siding, a single window, and no porch visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, simple railing, and a recessed main door
